content:
A while ago rustfmtcheck was passing. After a pull/rebase, rustfmt now reports a diff:

-            unsafe { bindings::no_hash_pointers = true; }
+            unsafe {
+                bindings::no_hash_pointers = true;
+            }

Is this multi-line unsafe block acceptable per our coding style guidelines?
If write it as unsafe { bindings::no_hash_pointers = true };, rustfmtcheck won’t complain.

Alvin Sun said:

If write it as unsafe { bindings::no_hash_pointers = true };, rustfmtcheck won’t complain.

that's the appropriate way
or was it the other way, i forgot :smile:
I remember someone once told me that code like this should stay on one line. So I’m here to double-check.
for function calls, we do the semi colon outside. For assignments I'm not sure
I think semi colon outside is good here too.
Thanks, everyone! Then I’ll put the semicolon outside.:blush:
Alvin Sun has marked this topic as resolved.
